info.title: PathFinder Backend - RAG Documents API
info.description: OpenAPI spec for PathFinder Backend RAG document management. Import this file or the served URL into Apidog to test JSON and PDF upload RAG flows.

post.description: Creates a users row, creates a matching profiles row using lookup values, and returns accessToken/refreshToken. The accessToken payload currently contains userId, email, and role.

post.summary: Upload and analyze a PDF CV
post.description: Requires Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> from auth register/login. Uploads the PDF to the private cvs bucket, parses text, fetches register/profile data, fetches RAG context for cv_analysis, calls OpenAI, saves cv_analyses, normalizes skills, and returns cv plus analysis.

413.description: Uploaded file exceeds the configured size limit

get.summary: Get my latest completed CV analysis

200.description: Latest CV analysis fetched successfully, or upload_cv action if no completed analysis exists.

get.summary: Get Profile for the current user

profile.headline: Backend Developer in Training
profile.bio: ITI graduate building strong fundamentals in Node.js, PostgreSQL, and API design.

profile.headline: Junior Frontend Developer
profile.bio: Computer science graduate focused on React, UI quality, and building practical portfolio projects.

patch.summary: Update User by the Admin only
Deactivate the User by only Admin
Activate the User by only Admin

example.description: Developed REST APIs using Node.js and Express.

details.message: "job_title" length must be at least 2 characters long
"company_name" length must be at least 2 characters long

experience.description: Developed REST APIs using Node.js and Express.
Developed REST APIs using Node.js and Express.

get.summary: Get the  education for the current user

example.description: Graduated with honors. Completed a graduation project focused on building an interactive, real-time application using React and Firebase.

data.description: Graduated with honors. Completed a graduation project focused on building an interactive, real-time application using React and Firebase.

post.description: Creates a RAG document from JSON text content, stores it in rag_documents, chunks the content, and stores chunks in rag_chunks.

example.title: CV Analysis Rules
example.content: Rules for analyzing and scoring CVs. Check clarity, relevant skills, education, experience, measurable achievements, ATS readability, and missing keywords.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter documents by AI feature type.

post.description: Uploads a PDF to Supabase Storage bucket rag-documents, assigns it to cv_analysis by default, extracts text with pdf-parse, stores document metadata/content, and creates chunks.

413.description: Uploaded file exceeds the configured size limit

get.summary: Get one RAG document with chunks

patch.description: Updates document metadata. If content changes, old chunks are deleted and recreated.

delete.description: Soft deletes a RAG document by setting is_active to false.

post.description: Generates a personalized learning roadmap after the authenticated user has a completed CV analysis. If forceRegenerate is false and an active roadmap exists, the existing roadmap is returned. If no completed CV analysis exists, returns requiredAction upload_cv.

200.description: Existing active roadmap returned, or upload_cv action if CV analysis is missing.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's latest active roadmap in the mobile-friendly response shape. If no roadmap exists, returns hasRoadmap false with requiredAction generate_roadmap or upload_cv.

200.description: Roadmap fetched successfully, or required action returned.

get.description: Returns a roadmap only when it belongs to the authenticated user.

404.description: Roadmap not found or does not belong to the authenticated user

patch.description: Updates the step and aggregate roadmap progress atomically, then returns the complete updated roadmap. isCompleted is optional; when supplied it must be true exactly when progress is 100.

404.description: Roadmap or roadmap step not found

post.description: Admin-only endpoint. Detects provider/external_id, checks duplicate imports, fetches public course metadata when possible, uses RAG context type course_analysis, runs AI metadata extraction, and returns matched/unmatched skills for admin review. AI does not decide user visibility.

metadata.title: database Fundamentals
metadata.description: Learn the basics of building   database .

200.description: Course import preview generated successfully, duplicate detected, or manual metadata required.

403.description: Authenticated user is not an admin

post.description: Admin-only endpoint. Saves the reviewed course as approved and stores selected course_skills mappings. Matched skills may use ai_analysis, admin_manual, or imported_metadata source values.

metadata.title: database Fundamentals
metadata.description: Learn the basics of building database.

analysis.summary: A foundational course covering the basics of database construction.

403.description: Authenticated user is not an admin

get.description: Protected endpoint. Returns deterministic course recommendations based on latest completed CV analysis missing skills, active roadmap steps, target career skills, and known user/CV skills. Only approved active courses are returned, and course_skills must have confidence >= 0.6 unless source is admin_manual.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of recommendations to return. Default 10, max 50.

example.message: How can I improve my CV for a UI UX role?

questions.question: What is the primary architectural purpose of implementing a 'Repository Pattern' in a clean architecture backend?
options: To enforce strict database schema validation
To decouple the business logic from the data access layer
To increase the speed of raw SQL execution
To automatically generate REST API endpoints

questions.question: In a high-concurrency Node.js environment, what is the risk of performing synchronous file I/O operations?
options: Memory leakage in the V8 heap
Blocking the main event loop and stalling other requests
Increased CPU usage due to context switching

questions.question: What is the main benefit of using 'Database Transactions' with ACID properties in a banking application?
options: Ensuring data integrity by rolling back if any part of the operation fails
Increasing the total number of connections allowed to the server
Compressing the transaction logs to save disk space

questions.question: When using Redis for caching, what is the most efficient way to handle 'Cache Stampede'?
options: Setting a very short Time-To-Live (TTL) for all keys
Implementing mutex locks or probabilistic early recomputation
Increasing the Redis node memory limit
Switching from Redis to an in-memory application variable

questions.question: What is the purpose of a 'Dead Letter Queue' (DLQ) in a message-driven architecture?
options: To store messages that could not be processed successfully
To prioritize high-latency tasks in the system
To act as a backup for the main message broker
To delete messages that have reached their expiration time

questions.question: Why is it considered a best practice to use environment variables for sensitive configuration like API keys?
options: To speed up the application startup time
To prevent secrets from being committed to version control systems
To allow multiple instances to share the same configuration
To make the application code easier to debug

questions.question: What happens when an asynchronous function in Node.js throws an error that is not caught by a try-catch block?
options: It triggers an 'unhandledRejection' event, potentially crashing the process
The error is automatically logged to the standard output and ignored
The event loop restarts the function automatically

questions.question: Which HTTP method should ideally be used for partial updates to a resource?
In PostgreSQL, what is the primary role of a 'Materialized View'?
options: To store the result of a query physically for faster read access
To provide a real-time interface for data insertion
To act as a temporary table during complex joins

questions.question: What is the 'N+1 query problem' in the context of ORMs?
options: Executing N queries to fetch associated data instead of one efficient query
A deadlock issue occurring when N transactions access 1 row
A performance bottleneck caused by too many indexes on a table
The overhead of opening N connections for 1 database operation

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using 'WebSockets' over 'Long Polling' for real-time applications?
options: Reduced overhead due to persistent bi-directional communication
Higher compatibility with legacy proxy servers
Simplified authentication flow for the user
Native support for binary data without base64 encoding

questions.question: What is 'Graceful Shutdown' in a server application?
options: Terminating all connections immediately to prevent resource leaks
Closing existing connections and finishing active requests before exiting
Restarting the server automatically when memory usage is high
Flushing the cache to the database before the process dies

questions.question: What is the main function of 'Middleware' in a framework like Express.js?
options: To perform tasks such as logging, authentication, or request parsing during the request cycle
To manage the connection pool of the database
To compile TypeScript code into JavaScript
To provide a graphical user interface for the server

questions.question: Which of these is a common strategy to mitigate 'Denial of Service' (DoS) attacks at the application level?
options: Increasing the timeout for all API requests
Implementing Rate Limiting for IP addresses
Disabling all error logging in production
Using a monolithic architecture to simplify traffic monitoring

questions.question: What does the 'Eventual Consistency' model imply in distributed databases?
options: Data will eventually be consistent across all nodes after a period of time
Data is consistent immediately upon writing
The system will reject writes if nodes are out of sync
Data consistency is only guaranteed if the system is restarted

questions.question: What is the purpose of a 'Load Balancer' health check?
options: To monitor the CPU temperature of the server
To verify if the server instance is capable of handling incoming traffic
To clear the temporary session files on the server
To update the operating system security patches

questions.question: Which design pattern is most suitable for creating complex objects step-by-step?
What is the primary reason for using 'Containerization' (e.g., Docker) in backend development?
options: To ensure consistent behavior across different environments
To eliminate the need for writing unit tests
To increase the speed of JavaScript execution
To provide a built-in database management system

questions.question: What is the benefit of 'Pagination' when returning a large dataset from an API?
options: Reducing payload size and improving client-side performance
Ensuring the database is always sorted alphabetically
Preventing concurrent access to the database
Increasing the security of the API endpoint

questions.question: Why is 'Hashing' passwords with a salt safer than using simple encryption?
options: Hashing is irreversible, whereas encryption is reversible
Hashing is significantly faster than encryption
Hashing allows for easier password recovery

session.quick_ai_insight: Significant technical preparation required across SQL, Python, and statistics to meet data analyst standards.

feedback_text.summary: This interview session indicates that you are at an early stage of your technical preparation. While the current results are low, this is a valuable baseline to identify specific knowledge gaps that can be closed with focused study.
strengths: Willingness to engage with technical assessment platforms.
Identified SQL clause confusion as an immediate area to revisit.
recommendations: Review the HAVING vs. WHERE clause distinction in SQL immediately.
Complete a structured Data Analysis roadmap focusing on SQL and Pandas.
Practice answering technical questions aloud before attempting timed assessments.
Prioritize understanding core concepts before moving to advanced machine learning topics.
areas_for_improvement: Statistical literacy (correlation, p-values, and outlier handling).

questions.question: Which SQL clause is used to filter aggregated data after a GROUP BY operation?
questions.feedback: The selected option is not correct for this interview question.
questions.ai_suggestion: Review the concept and be ready to explain why the correct option fits better.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a LEFT JOIN in a relational database?
options: Returns only matching rows in both tables
Returns all rows from the left table and matched rows from the right
Returns all rows from both tables
Returns rows only from the right table
questions.ai_suggestion: Revisit this topic and prepare a short, confident explanation.

questions.question: In data cleaning, what is the best approach to handle missing values in a numerical column with significant outliers?
Which Python library is most commonly used for high-performance data manipulation and analysis using DataFrames?
What does a correlation coefficient of -0.9 indicate in a dataset?
What is the primary purpose of normalizing data before performing clustering analysis?
options: To reduce the number of features
To ensure all variables contribute equally to the distance calculation
To remove outliers from the dataset
To convert categorical data into numerical data

questions.question: Which chart type is most effective for visualizing the distribution of a single continuous variable?
What is the main difference between a Primary Key and a Unique Key in a database table?
options: Unique key allows null values, Primary key does not
Primary key can have duplicates, Unique key cannot
Unique key is for foreign relations

questions.question: Which statistical measure is most robust to extreme outliers in a dataset?
In the context of data pipelines, what is an 'ETL' process?
What is the purpose of the 'Pandas melt' function?
options: To reshape a DataFrame from wide to long format
To filter rows based on conditions
To calculate the mean of columns

questions.question: When performing a hypothesis test, what does a p-value less than 0.05 typically signify?
options: Failing to reject the null hypothesis

questions.question: Which join type would you use to get all records from both tables, including those that do not have a match?
What is the primary advantage of using a columnar database for analytical queries?
options: Efficient reading of specific columns over large datasets

questions.question: Which Python library would you choose for creating interactive data dashboards?
What is 'Simpson's Paradox' in data analysis?
options: When a trend appears in subgroups but disappears when combined
When two variables are perfectly correlated
When the sample size is too small

questions.question: How do you handle categorical variables in a machine learning model?
options: Convert to numerical via One-Hot Encoding
Only use them for target variables

questions.question: In SQL, which function is used to count the number of non-null values in a column?
What is the difference between a 'Join' and a 'Union' in SQL?
options: Join combines columns, Union combines rows
Join combines rows, Union combines columns
Join is only for numerical data

questions.question: What is the objective of 'Feature Engineering' in a data project?
options: To create new input variables to improve model performance

User.description: Sanitized users row returned from login. password_hash is not included.

forceRegenerate.description: When false, returns the existing active roadmap if one exists. When true, pauses existing active roadmaps and generates a new one.

isCompleted.description: Optional. If omitted, it is derived from progress. If supplied, it must be true exactly when progress is 100 and false otherwise.

description.example: Start with types, interfaces, functions, and React component typing.
Personalized roadmap based on the user's CV analysis and target career.
